extraction.one_line_summary: LEÓN SÁNCHEZ attacked with 12km remaining on final climb sequence, held solo to win as SCREEUWS took GC lead by narrowest margin
tactical_patterns: Astana forced pace on final two climbs (last 35km) to set up selection rather than defending GC fractions.
Spanish veteran León Sánchez capitalized on forced pace with 12km attack, suggesting late-stage positioning over early aggression.

rider_signals.LEÓN SÁNCHEZ Luis: positive: first Tour de Suisse win off forced pace suggests improved late-race positioning in multi-climb finales
rider_signals.SCREEUWS Kasper: positive: took GC lead by fractions despite not winning, indicating bonus seconds awareness and positioning discipline
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: negative: lost bonus seconds to Parrish at intermediate sprint despite GC being decided by fractions
rider_signals.IMHOFF Claudio: neutral: track specialist's mechanical on solo break confirms unsuitability for sustained breakaway efforts
